title: Mesh updates in an extended reality environment
abstract: Various implementations or examples set forth a method for scanning a three-dimensional (3D) environment. The method includes generating a 3D representation of the 3D environment that includes one or more 3D meshes. The method also includes determining at least a portion of the 3D environment that falls within a current frame captured by the image sensor. The method further includes generating one or more additional 3D meshes representing the at least a portion of the 3D environment and combining the one or more additional 3D meshes with the one or more 3D meshes into an update to the 3D representation of the 3D environment.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1 . A method for scanning a three-dimensional (3D) environment, comprising: generating a 3D representation of the 3D environment that includes one or more 3D meshes, wherein the one or more 3D meshes comprise (i) a first set of vertices representing 3D points captured of the 3D environment by a depth sensor on a host device, (ii) a first set of faces formed from edges between pairs of vertices in the first set of vertices, and (iii) a first set of textures generated based on one or more previous frames captured of the 3D environment by an image sensor on the host device; determining at least a portion of the 3D environment that falls within a current frame captured by the image sensor; generating one or more additional 3D meshes representing the at least a portion of the 3D environment, wherein the one or more additional 3D meshes comprise (i) a second set of vertices representing additional 3D points captured by the depth sensor concurrently with capture of the current frame by the image sensor, (ii) a second set of faces formed from edges between pairs of vertices in the second set of vertices, and (iii) a second set of textures generated based on the current frame; and combining the one or more additional 3D meshes with the one or more 3D meshes into an update to the 3D representation of the 3D environment. 2. The method of claim 1 , further comprising transmitting the one or more additional 3D meshes for receipt by one or more remote devices executing an extended reality application that renders the 3D representation on the one or more remote devices. 3. The method of claim 1 , wherein determining the at least a portion of the 3D environment that falls within the current frame comprises projecting one or more 

vertices of the one or more 3D meshes onto one or more pixel locations in the current frame. 4. The method of claim 1 , wherein combining the one or more additional 3D meshes with the one or more 3D meshes comprises discarding one or more portions of the one or more 3D meshes that overlap with the one or more additional 3D meshes to trigger replacement of the one or more portions with the one or more additional 3D meshes in the 3D representation. 5. The method of claim 1 , wherein combining the one or more additional 3D meshes with the one or more 3D meshes comprises overlaying one or more untextured portions of the one or more 3D meshes with the one or more additional 3D meshes. 6. A computing device, comprising: a memory that includes an application; and a processor that is coupled to the memory, and when executing the application, performs the steps of: generating, based on sensor data captured by a depth sensor on a device, a 3D mesh representing a physical space; dividing the 3D mesh into a plurality of sub-meshes, wherein each of the plurality of sub-meshes comprises a corresponding set of vertices and a corresponding set of faces comprising edges between pairs of vertices; determining that at least a portion of a first sub-mesh in the plurality of sub-meshes is in a current frame captured by an image sensor on the device; and updating the 3D mesh by texturing the at least a portion of the first submesh with one or more pixels in the current frame onto which the first sub-mesh is projected. 7. The computing device of claim 6, wherein the processor further performs transmitting a stream comprising the updated 3D mesh to a distributed messaging 

system for receipt by one or more remote devices that render the 3D mesh in an augmented reality environment. 8. The computing device of claim 6, wherein dividing the 3D mesh into the plurality of sub-meshes comprises populating a plurality of face arrays representing the plurality of sub-meshes with disjoint subsets of faces in the 3D mesh, wherein each of the plurality of face arrays comprises an ordering of a subset of faces from a face array for the 3D mesh. 9. The computing device of claim 6, wherein determining that the at least a portion of the first sub-mesh is in the current frame comprises determining that the first sub-mesh includes at least one vertex that falls within the current frame. 10. The computing device of claim 6, wherein determining that the at least a portion of the first sub-mesh is in the current frame comprises determining that all vertices of the first sub-mesh fall within the current frame. 11 . The computing device of claim 6, wherein determining that the at least a portion of the first sub-mesh is in the current frame comprises projecting one or more vertices of the first sub-mesh onto one or more pixel locations in the current frame. 12. The computing device of claim 6, wherein texturing the at least a portion of the first sub-mesh comprises assigning one or more texture coordinates to one or more vertices of the first sub-mesh based on projection of the one or more vertices to the one or more pixels in the current frame. 13. The computing device of claim 6, wherein updating the 3D mesh comprises discarding an older version of the first sub-mesh that is textured using a previous frame captured by the image sensor. 14. The computing device of claim 6, wherein updating the 3D mesh comprises overlaying an untextured version of the first sub-mesh with the textured at least a portion of the first sub-mesh. 15. The computing device of claim 6, wherein the plurality of sub-meshes are contiguous. 16. A method comprising generating, based on sensor data captured by a depth sensor on a device, one or more 3D meshes representing a physical space, wherein each of the one or more 3D meshes comprises a corresponding set of vertices and a corresponding set of faces comprising edges between pairs of vertices; determining that a mesh in the one or more 3D meshes is visible in a current frame captured by an image sensor on the device; determining, based on the corresponding set of vertices and the corresponding set of faces for the mesh, a portion of the mesh that lies within a view frustum associated with the current frame; and updating the one or more 3D meshes by texturing the portion of the mesh with one or more pixels in the current frame onto which the portion is projected. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein determining that the mesh is visible in the current frame comprises: generating a grid of 2D points in the current frame; generating a set of rays representing projection of the grid of 2D points into a 3D space in which the sets of vertices of the one or more 3D meshes are distributed; determining at least a subset of the one or more 3D meshes that intersect the set of rays; and identifying the mesh in the at least a subset of the one or more 3D meshes as visible in the current frame based on a proximity of the mesh to the image sensor. 18. The method of claim 16, wherein determining the portion of the mesh that lies within the view frustum comprises: 

 grouping faces in the mesh into a first set of faces that is fully outside the view frustum, a second set of faces that is fully inside the view frustum, and a third set of faces that intersect the view frustum; and dividing the third set of faces by one or more planes bounding the view frustum to generate a fourth set of faces that is fully outside the view frustum or fully inside the view frustum. 19. The method of claim 16, wherein determining the portion of the mesh that lies within the view frustum comprises: determining one or more faces in the mesh that intersect the view frustum; calculating intersection points of the one or more faces with a plane representing a boundary of the view frustum; and forming one or more new faces using the intersection points and one or more vertices of the one or more faces. 20. The method of claim 16, wherein updating the one or more 3D meshes comprises: creating a clone of an untextured version of the mesh, wherein the clone includes the portion of the mesh that lies within the view frustum and is textured with the one or more pixels in the current frame; and overlaying an untextured version of the mesh with the clone.
